After rotating (SPACE), the white shape now has red blocks on the LEFT side. Looking at the image, I can see the white shape is now oriented with the opening facing LEFT, and the red blocks are on the left side. The green shape still has red blocks on its LEFT side.
Let me check the current positions and see if the red blocks align better now.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after rotation
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N', 'R']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows[{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}] cols[{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}]")

print("\nAdjacency (non-background):")
for pair in seg['adjacency_list']:
    i, j = pair
    ci = next(n['color'] for n in nodes if n['id'] == i)
    cj = next(n['color'] for n in nodes if n['id'] == j)
    if ci != 'S' or cj != 'S':
        print(f"  {i}({ci}) - {j}({cj})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
